questions.question: After going live with an Adobe Real-Time CDP implementation, a data architect notices that a field defined in field group A should actually be part of field group B. How can this issue be resolved?

Pseudonymous profiles cannot qualify for same-page personalization in Adobe RT-CDP
correct: Known and pseudonymous profiles can use same-page personalization with edge segmentation
questions.reason: Edge segmentation supports low-latency qualification for both known and pseudonymous visitors in this scenario.

questions.question: A healthcare organization needs home-page web analytics to remain non-RHD, authenticated data linked to a healthcare identifier to be treated as RHD, and on-site personalization to use only non-RHD data. How should the platform be configured?
answers: Configure one sandbox and use data retention policies to separate RHD and non-RHD data
Create one sandbox with two datasets, one labeled as RHD and one unlabeled, and create audiences using only the non-RHD dataset
Create one sandbox and apply the RHD label only to the known customer identifiers
Create two sandboxes, one to collect RHD data and one to collect non-RHD data, and connect the personalization tool to the non-RHD sandbox
correct: Create two sandboxes, one to collect RHD data and one to collect non-RHD data, and connect the personalization tool to the non-RHD sandbox
questions.reason: Separate sandboxes cleanly isolate restricted healthcare data from the non-RHD data used for personalization.

questions.question: What is the primary function of profile assembly in Adobe Real-Time Customer Data Platform?
answers: Exporting data to external destinations
Creating static customer segments for campaigns
Performing probabilistic identity resolution exclusively
Resolving multiple datasets into a unified customer profile
correct: Resolving multiple datasets into a unified customer profile
questions.reason: Profile assembly combines profile and event data into a single unified customer view.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) in Adobe RT-CDP?
answers: Restricting data access based on user roles
Assigning data access based on specific attributes of the data
Enabling batch processing for customer profiles
Automating identity resolution processes
correct: Assigning data access based on specific attributes of the data
questions.reason: ABAC uses attributes of data, resources, or context to determine access permissions.

questions.question: A customer notices incorrect data was loaded into Real-Time Customer Profile for only two profiles from a single XDM Individual Profile dataset. What action can correct the problem?
answers: Look up each profile in the UI and update the incorrect attributes manually
Delete the entire dataset containing the incorrect profiles
Re-load all the affected profiles with the correct data and have the profile update and/or replace the existing records in the dataset
Delete the batch that created the problem from the dataset in the data lake and let the profile roll back to its previous state
correct: Re-load all the affected profiles with the correct data and have the profile update and/or replace the existing records in the dataset
questions.reason: For a small number of bad records, reloading corrected records is the recommended fix rather than deleting the whole dataset.

questions.question: A company wants to analyze an audience before activating it to an upcoming email campaign. What is the recommended approach to extract this audience?
answers: Create a segment, run the segment export job, and share the audience through dataset export
Create a query via data distiller and extract the resulting audience
Create a segment and configure a destination to extract the audience
Extract the audience using a custom HTTP API
correct: Create a segment and configure a destination to extract the audience
questions.reason: Audience extraction for activation analysis is done through segment creation and destination configuration.

questions.question: Which type of attributes or fields can be exported to batch destinations in Real-Time CDP?
answers: Individual profile class-based schema attributes
Custom class-based schema attributes
Experience event class-based schema attributes
Pseudonymous profile class-based schema attributes
correct: Individual profile class-based schema attributes
questions.reason: Batch destinations export profile attributes from profile-class schemas rather than raw event-class fields.

questions.question: A data engineer is bringing in an external list of audience memberships to Adobe Experience Platform. Which two XDM schema classes should be used to create the schemas? (Choose two.)
answers: Profile Definition
XDM Individual Profile
Segment Definition
XDM ExperienceEvent
correct: XDM Individual Profile
Segment Definition
questions.reason: The audience membership import scenario in the PDF uses XDM Individual Profile together with Segment Definition.
